description_long: This bottle has a highly stylized, organic form reminiscent of a serpent or dragon, with a long, curved neck and an open mouth at the top. The surface is metallic with a mottled, dark bronze and gold coloration, giving it an aged, weathered appearance. The body is rounded and asymmetrical, with subtle ridges and bulges that enhance its sculptural quality. The mouth of the bottle is wide and slightly open, suggesting it could function as a receptacle, though no cap or closure is visible. The finish is somewhat reflective but marred by patches of darker discoloration, adding to its antique look. There are no visible labels, branding, or decorative text on the exterior.
description: A metallic, serpent-shaped bottle with a curved neck, open mouth, and mottled bronze-gold surface, featuring an aged, weathered finish.
description_view_0: Front view shows the open mouth and the full curve of the neck, with the rounded body and mottled metallic surface clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the elongated, arched neck and the open mouth, with the body’s asymmetrical bulges and surface texture more pronounced.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the rounded body and the smooth, continuous curve of the neck, with less detail on the mouth.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizes the neck’s curve and the open mouth, with the mottled metallic finish and body contours visible.
